Author: Eryk Orlowski
Message:
feat: Configure new production droplet and update documentation

- New droplet: 152.42.139.18 (2vCPU, 4GB RAM, Ubuntu 24.04)
- Docker 28.3.1 and Docker Compose 1.29.2 installed
- Security configured: UFW firewall, fail2ban
- Updated all documentation with new IP and credentials
- Created check-droplet.sh script for monitoring
- Prepared GitLab CI/CD variables documentation

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Eryk Orlowski
Message:
Complete Task 3: Comprehensive visualization package for benchmark results

Added complete visualization suite showcasing 98.4% framework optimization:

## Visualization Assets Created:
- Interactive HTML charts (startup times, performance radar, dependency networks)
- Static images for presentations (waterfall charts, heatmaps, comparisons)
- Social media optimized exports (LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram formats)
- Professional documentation charts (before/after, improvement metrics)

## Key Visualizations:
- Size reduction: 595.7MB → 9.6MB (98.4% smaller)
- Startup improvement: 5.047s → 0.856s (83% faster)
- Memory optimization: 487.3MB → 42.1MB (91% less)
- Dependency reduction: 155 → 32 deps (79% fewer)

## Technical Implementation:
- 15+ professional charts in multiple formats
- Interactive networks using NetworkX and Pyvis
- Performance radar charts with Plotly
- Social media templates with platform-specific sizing
- Automated visualization generation scripts

## Content Marketing Ready:
- LinkedIn carousel templates (1080x1080)
- Twitter/X graphics (16:9 optimized)
- Instagram story formats (9:16)
- Copy templates and hashtag recommendations

This completes Week 0 Task 3 and provides comprehensive visual evidence
of LiteCrew optimization success for technical documentation and marketing.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Discovered a potential authorization token provided in a curl command header, which could compromise the curl accessed resource.
Author: Eryk Orlowski
Message:
Complete Block 1.1: Fork and Initial Cleanup

- Task 1.1.1: Forked CrewAI repository and created lite-personal branch
- Task 1.1.2: Removed all telemetry and analytics
  - Removed telemetry directory and imports
  - Removed OpenTelemetry dependencies
  - Created stub implementations where needed
- Task 1.1.3: Removed enterprise features
  - Removed authentication/deploy/organization/tools modules
  - Removed auth0-python dependency
  - Simplified to single-user local-only operation
  - Fixed remaining enterprise references

All validation scripts pass successfully.

  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key"
